title: Food Pantries * Mental Health Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Food > Food Pantries * Mental Health Issues 1 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Refine Results - Select - No Filter African American Community AIDS/HIV At Risk Youth Hispanic/Latino Community Homeless Families Homeless Youth Mental Health Issues Seasonal Workers Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF LIFEWAYS INC PEER SUPPORT CENTER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Partners with Lifeways to provide a range of services and support to mental health consumers, including self-help groups, skill groups, advocacy, activities, peer-to-peer support, food bank, linkage to community resources, dual diagnosis group, and National Alliance for Mental Illness (NAMI) services. 702 Sunset Drive Ontario, OR 97914 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing mental health issues Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 889-9167 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWAYS INC PEER SUPPORT CENTER
